name: Track & Field
description:
Track & Field is an arcade-style sports game centered around six Olympic events: the 100m sprint, long jump, javelin throw, 110m hurdles, hammer throw, and high jump. The game supports single-player and competitive multiplayer modes. The core gameplay mechanic revolves around precisely timed button presses to build speed and angle for the different events. Each event will have a different feel and rhythm, even though the control scheme is similar across events. 

In the 100m sprint, 110m hurdles, and hammer throw, players will rapidly alternate between two buttons to increase their runner's speed. For the 110m hurdles, a jump button will need to be pressed with appropriate timing to clear the hurdles without tripping. For the Hammer Throw, there will be a spin-up mechanic before the throw is executed using a separate button. 

The long jump, javelin throw, and high jump will also involve building up speed using two alternating buttons, but with the addition of an angle/takeoff control. For these events, the player will need to trigger a jump/throw at the right time and angle to achieve maximum distance/height. 

The success of each event depends on the speed built up and the timing of the jump/throw angle. Failure to achieve sufficient speed will impact the performance of the event. In multiplayer, players will compete directly against each other, displayed side-by-side on the screen. The highest score in each event wins.

controls.arrow_left: Button 1 for running/speed building. Angle adjustment for long jump, javelin, and high jump.
controls.arrow_right: Button 2 for running/speed building. Angle adjustment for long jump, javelin, and high jump.
controls.space: Jump for hurdles and high jump. Takeoff/throw action for long jump, javelin, and hammer throw.
controls.arrow_up: Angle adjustment for long jump, javelin, and high jump.
controls.arrow_down: Angle adjustment for long jump, javelin, and high jump.
